relations: Sir Sandford Fleming proposed the concept of time zones, which are now used worldwide to standardize time
Railway Time was an early form of standardized timekeeping that influenced the development of time zones
The expansion of global trade necessitated the creation of time zones to standardize time measurement across international borders
The development of transportation networks was a key factor in the need for the standardization of time, leading to the establishment of time zones
The inefficiency of local time for train schedules across long distances was a significant factor in the development of standardized time and time zones
The use of telegraphs and the need for synchronized communication further emphasized the need for standardized time zones
The internet has made coordinating time across different time zones easier, highlighting the importance of time zones in global communication
Global telecommunications networks facilitate real-time collaboration across time zones, underscoring the significance of time zones in international logistics
The Greenwich Meridian was chosen as the Prime Meridian at the International Meridian Conference
GMT was established as the reference point for time coordination at the International Meridian Conference
The International Meridian Conference was held in Washington, D.C.
The Greenwich Meridian is located in London
The United States implemented Daylight Saving Time following the development of time zones
Both the internet and global telecommunications networks facilitate instant communication and collaboration across different time zones
